qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I still encounter the "not enough hosts" error or need a particular availability zone when launching a VM?
qa_pairs.answer: If you still get this "not enough hosts" error, or there is a reason why you need a particular availability zone, send an email to cloud@tech.alliancecan.ca. Include the cloud name, project name, the steps you have taken thus far, and the reason why you need a particular availability zone if applicable.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if my virtual machine instance still won't boot after following the troubleshooting steps?
qa_pairs.answer: If you have followed the troubleshooting steps and your instance still can't boot, submit a ticket to cloud@tech.alliancecan.ca with your username, project name, cloud name, and any information you collected during troubleshooting.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find more information about contacting support and submitting tickets?
qa_pairs.answer: More information about contacting support and submitting tickets can be found on the Technical Support page.

qa_pairs.question: What is the first step to take if I cannot connect to my virtual machine or a service it's running?
qa_pairs.answer: If you cannot connect to your virtual machine or a service it's running, check the System Status page.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if the System Status page indicates an incident on my hosting cloud?
qa_pairs.answer: If there is an incident on your hosting cloud, wait until the incident is resolved, then try to connect again.

qa_pairs.question: What if there is no incident reported on the System Status page, but I still can't connect to my VM?
qa_pairs.answer: If there is no incident reported on the System Status page for the cloud hosting your project, try to log in to the OpenStack dashboard for your cloud project.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find the login link for my cloud's OpenStack dashboard?
qa_pairs.answer: Login links for different clouds can be found on the Cloud wiki page. For example, if your project is hosted at Arbutus, use https://arbutus.alliancecan.ca to log in.

qa_pairs.question: What should I check if I cannot reach the login page for my cloud?
qa_pairs.answer: If you cannot reach the login page for your cloud, verify that you have internet connectivity by trying to reach a website like https://www.google.com with a browser.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I have internet connectivity but still can't reach my cloud's login page?
qa_pairs.answer: If you have internet connectivity but cannot reach the login page for your cloud, submit a ticket to the cloud queue by emailing cloud@tech.alliancecan.ca. Include your name, username, hosting cloud, project name, and the steps you have taken thus far.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find more details about submitting support tickets?
qa_pairs.answer: For more on submitting tickets, see the Technical Support page.

qa_pairs.question: What if I can reach the cloud login page but can't log in?
qa_pairs.answer: If you are able to reach the login page for your cloud but cannot log in, please refer to the “Can’t login to Cloud” guide in the previous section on this page.

qa_pairs.question: How can I check if my virtual machine is running from the cloud dashboard?
qa_pairs.answer: Log in to your cloud dashboard, navigate to the 'Instances' screen on the left-side menu, and look at the 'Power State' for your VM. It should be 'Running'.

qa_pairs.question: What if my VM's Power State is not 'Running' in the cloud dashboard?
qa_pairs.answer: If your VM's Power State is not 'Running' (e.g., 'Shut Down'), try to restart it using the 'Actions' menu on the right-hand side. Select 'Start Instance' or 'Resume Instance' depending on the available options.

qa_pairs.question: How can I investigate why my VM was taken out of the running state?
qa_pairs.answer: From the 'Instances' screen, click on your instance name (VM name) and then click on the 'Action Log' tab. This will show all the actions that have been applied to your VM.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I find an unrecognized action in my VM's action logs?
qa_pairs.answer: If you find an action in your VM's action logs that you don’t recognize, contact support by emailing cloud@tech.alliancecan.ca for help to figure out what it was. Include your name, username, hosting cloud, project name, and the User ID from the action log for the action you want to investigate.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find the console log for my virtual machine?
qa_pairs.answer: The 'log' tab, found on the same screen as the 'Action Log' for your VM, will show you the console log.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I am unable to restart my virtual machine?
qa_pairs.answer: If you can't restart your VM, submit a ticket to the cloud queue by emailing cloud@tech.alliancecan.ca. Include your name, username, hosting cloud, project name, VM ID, the issue you are seeing, and the steps you have taken to troubleshoot it so far.

qa_pairs.question: How do I find my virtual machine's ID?
qa_pairs.answer: You can find the VM ID by clicking on your instance, then looking at the 'Overview' tab.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if my VM is running and I've followed the initial troubleshooting, but I still can't reach my application or web service on it?
qa_pairs.answer: If your VM is running and you have followed the initial steps but still can't reach your application or web service, try to connect using SSH (Secure Shell protocol).

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find instructions for connecting to my VM with SSH?
qa_pairs.answer: Instructions for connecting to your VM with SSH can be found in the Cloud Quick Start Guide, near the bottom of the page in the 'Connecting to your VM with SSH' section.

qa_pairs.question: If I get a login prompt when trying to SSH, what should I verify?
qa_pairs.answer: If you get a login prompt when trying to SSH, verify that you are using the correct key pair and username.

qa_pairs.question: How can I check which key pair I should be using for SSH?
qa_pairs.answer: To check which key pair you should be using for SSH, click on 'Compute' on the left-side menu of the OpenStack page, then 'Instances,' and look for the column 'Key Pair'.

qa_pairs.question: What are the default usernames for SSH connecting to VMs based on their operating system?
qa_pairs.answer: The default usernames for SSH are 'debian' for Debian, 'ubuntu' for Ubuntu, 'almalinux' for AlmaLinux, 'centos' for CentOS, and 'fedora' for Fedora.

qa_pairs.question: Does the default username table apply if I used a custom CloudInit script to change my username?
qa_pairs.answer: No, if you explicitly changed your username with a custom CloudInit script, the default username table does not apply; the correct username will be what you changed it to.
